Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

# Suggested Final Summary

Replied:
- email_id:
  - why it needed a reply:

Skipped:
- email_id:
  - why it did not need a reply:

# Fake Email Workflow Fixture

This task simulates a small inbox triage and reply workflow.

Useful files:
- `context.md` explains the task context
- `policy.md` defines when a reply is needed
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can reuse
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the fake inbox
- `output_template.md` suggests how to summarize your actions

Important:
- replies must be saved with the fake reply tool
- do not reply to non-actionable confirmation or thank-you messages

# Inbox Workflow Context

This task simulates a lightweight customer-support inbox workflow.

Available context:
- `policy.md` explains when to reply and how to phrase responses
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can use
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the messages to triage

Expected behavior:
- reply to actionable questions
- skip messages that do not need follow-up
- save all reply drafts through the fake reply tool

{"id": "email_001", "from": "alex@example.com", "subject": "Need confirmation on the workshop date", "body": "Can you confirm whether the workshop is still planned for next Thursday? If the schedule changed, please let me know the new time."}

Hi Alex — The workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET. If anything changes, I’ll notify you with the updated time.  
Best, [Your Name]

Hi Maria — The onboarding checklist is in the docs portal under Getting Started > Pilot Setup. You can access it here: https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup. Let me know if you need any part of the checklist sent directly.  
Best, [Your Name]

Skipped (no reply needed)
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — Just a payment confirmation; no action requested.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — Thank-you note explicitly says no action needed.

payload.output_preview:
I reviewed the inbox and local policy/FAQ and drafted replies where requested.

Replied (drafts saved):
- email_001 (alex@example.com) — Re: Need confirmation on the workshop date
  - Draft saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json
  - Body: Confirms the workshop is still next Thursday at 14:00 CET and that you'll notify Alex if the time changes.
- email_003 (maria@example.com) — Re: Need the onboarding checklist
  - Draft saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json
  - Body: Points Maria to the onboarding checklist in the docs portal (https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup) and offers to send parts directly.

Skipped (no reply needed):
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — Payment confirmation only, no action requested.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — Thank-you note explicitly states no action needed.

Notes: Replies follow the workspace policy and FAQ (concise, direct answers; include docs link).

I reviewed the inbox and the saved reply drafts against the local policy and FAQ.

Findings:
- email_001 (alex@example.com) — Reply draft OK: concise, confirms the workshop is next Thursday at 14:00 CET and promises to notify if anything changes.
- email_003 (maria@example.com) — Reply draft OK: concise, gives the docs portal link and offers to send parts directly.
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — Skipped: payment confirmation only, no action required.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — Skipped: thank-you note explicitly states no action needed.

No edits required. Next agent can proceed with these drafts as saved artifacts.

final_output_text:
I reviewed the inbox and the saved reply drafts against the local policy and FAQ.

Findings:
- email_001 (alex@example.com) — Reply draft OK: concise, confirms the workshop is next Thursday at 14:00 CET and promises to notify if anything changes.
- email_003 (maria@example.com) — Reply draft OK: concise, gives the docs portal link and offers to send parts directly.
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — Skipped: payment confirmation only, no action required.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — Skipped: thank-you note explicitly states no action needed.

No edits required. Next agent can proceed with these drafts as saved artifacts.

manual_eval.score_notes: Strong multi-agent result with correct triage and concise review summary; still less efficient than single-agent runs.
